title: January Meetup: We use AI, but are we using it right?
description:
AI is no longer just about prompts and demos — it’s about **building real systems that think, act, and scale responsibly**.

Join **Mumbai Women Coders (MWC)** for our January Meetup, where we explore how modern AI is being used in real-world products, teams, and workflows. This session is designed to spark conversations around **practical AI, automation, product thinking, and ethical responsibility**, all in a welcoming, community-driven environment.

Whether you’re experimenting with AI tools, building AI-powered products, or simply curious about how AI is shaping the future of technology, this meetup offers insights, shared experiences, and meaningful connections.

Whether you’re a developer, product manager, student, founder, or tech enthusiast, this meetup offers a space to **learn, exchange ideas, and connect with like-minded professionals**, especially within a supportive women-in-tech community.

📆 January 31, 2026
📍Venue: Microsoft Office, Kalina, Santacruz East
Join Group: [https://chat.whatsapp.com/Izg9LXqH4996RYJpuWFgZc](https://chat.whatsapp.com/Izg9LXqH4996RYJpuWFgZc)

Expect engaging discussions, real examples, and honest conversations; not just theory, but what actually works (and what doesn’t).

Come learn, connect, and grow with a community that believes in **building tech with purpose**. 🚀

venue.name: Microsoft Corporation
venue.address: 4th Floor, Windsor, Off CST Road, Kalina, Santacruz East, MMRDA Area, Kalina, Santacruz East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400098, India

title: TechSphere
description:
**Welcome to TechSphere 2025 – Where Innovation Meets Inspiration!** 🚀
Get ready for a day packed with knowledge, networking, and a sprinkle of fun. **TechSphere 2025** is not just another tech event—it’s where ideas turn into actions, and passionate minds come together to shape the future.
**Here's what to expect:**

* 🎙️ Speaker Sessions – Hear from tech leaders, innovators, and game-changers.
* 💬 Panel Discussions – Engage in meaningful conversations about trends and challenges in tech.
* 🤝 Networking Opportunities – Meet like-minded professionals, industry experts, and maybe even your future collaborator!

**What to Bring:**

* An open mind and lots of curiosity.
* A notebook or device to jot down those golden insights.
* Your LinkedIn app—trust us, you’ll need it.
* A smile (because who doesn’t love a friendly face?)

**Finding Us:**
We’ll be in the heart of Mumbai—details coming soon! Look for the group that’s buzzing with ideas and maybe a little too excited about coffee breaks. ☕
Whether you're here to learn, connect, or just soak in the vibes, TechSphere promises to leave you inspired. Mark your calendar (22nd Feb) and get ready to be part of something big! 🌟

https://chat.whatsapp.com/JWsDYjjaf3OAYRkLK0dhJ5

title: Join Tomorrow for GenAI Lab Workshop by Women X Code
description:
## Details

Join tomorrow **Women X Code** for an engaging and enlightening workshop led by Megha Gupta. Megha is a PhD in robotics from USC and a strong background in AI applications in mental health and a passion for education. This workshop will delve into the fascinating world of LLM's, offering hands-on experience in building with them.
**Women X Code**
This is a community dedicated to help each one of us succeed in technology careers. We are dedicated to increasing the representation of diverse women at every levels - students and interns to CTO's and board Members.
**Why You Should Attend:**

* **Learn from a Leading Expert**: Megha Gupta, with her rich academic background and professional experience—including her role as the former AI lead at Wysa—is an amazing person to learn from.
* **Hands-On Experience**: This workshop isn't just about listening; it's about doing. You'll get a chance to build your own Rag-Enabled Chatbot under the guidance of an expert.
* **Networking Opportunities**: Meet and connect with other like-minded professionals and enthusiasts in the tech community. Expand your professional network in a supportive and inspiring environment.
* **Empowerment Through Technology**: Women X Code is dedicated to empowering women in technology. This workshop is a great opportunity to gain valuable skills and confidence in a field that is shaping the future.

**Who Should Attend:** This workshop is ideal for anyone interested in AI, robotics, and software development, regardless of gender. Whether you are a student, a professional looking to shift to AI, or simply an enthusiast eager to learn about the latest in tech, this workshop will provide valuable knowledge and skills.
**Additional Details:**

* Snacks and refreshments will be provided.
* Please bring a laptop for hands-on activities.
* Free Wi-Fi available at the venue.

**Registration Information:**

* **Fee**: Free (registration required)
* **RSVP**: Ensure your spot by registering through this page. Limited seats available!

**Join tomorrow for a day of learning, networking, and innovation at Women X Code. Empower yourself with cutting-edge skills and make meaningful connections in the tech world. We look forward to seeing you there!**

title: Mumbai Women Coders July Meetup
description:
**🚀Join Us at Mumbai Women Coders July Meetup! 🚀**

The MWC Team is thrilled to invite you to our upcoming Tech-Meetup, an event designed for tech enthusiasts, innovators, and professionals. Mark your calendars and get ready for an inspiring day of learning, networking, and collaboration.

📅 **Date:** July 06, 2024
🕒 **Time:** 11:00 AM - 2:30 PM IST
📍 **Location:** [ITM IDM Mumbai Office](https://www.google.com/maps/place/ITM+IDM+-+Fashion+Designing+%26+Interior+Design,+Animation+%26+VFX+College+in+Mumbai/@19.1279099,72.84686,17z/data=!3m1!4b1!4m6!3m5!1s0x3be7b640618856d3:0xa0798149162a3c3f!8m2!3d19.1279099!4d72.84686!16s%2Fg%2F11g_qjz0zd?entry=ttu)

**Speaker Details:**

1. Megha Pandey, System Administrator will be speaking about **Quantum Computing \~A session beyond AI**

Through this talk, we will learn about the most popular emerging technology Quantum Computing. The more you learn, the more will be your curiosity to know more about Quantum and its uses in Computer science. Unleashed ideas and principles of Quantum Computing will help you understand technologies that even exist beyond AI.

A complete beginner-friendly session with examples and indeed with a new programming language. By the end of this talk, you will be able to

1. Understand and explain Quantum Computing.
2. Learn about the principles of Quantum Physics and how it helps to do computers' most powerful calculations.
3. Learn about a new programming language

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
2\. Manali Raut\, Software Development Engineer \(SDE\) II will be speaking about **"Design Thinking Transforms Code into User-Centric Innovation"**

The presentation will cover topics-

* What is Design Thinking? How does it impact the lifecycle of software development?
* What is Innovation vs Operation worlds, along with use-cases
* 4 phases of Innovation & how to implement these phases using frameworks

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
**Venue Partners:** https://letsupgrade.in/

**How to Register :**

*RSVP here on the meetup page.*

**Admission:** Free for all attendees!

Join us for an exciting event filled with knowledge sharing and community building. We look forward to seeing you there!

📧For any inquiries, please tweet us [@WomenCoders01](@WomenCoders01) 🚀

You can also reach out to the team members below:
1)Manjula Dube [Manjula](https://x.com/manjula_dube)
2)Amtulla Baranwala [Amtulla](https://x.com/amtulladev)
3)Disha Mourya [Disha](https://x.com/disha4mourya)

title: Lets get together for another online meet up - August
description:
Our event will be live streamed: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_23dScqH0yQ

Meet our first speaker
Rohini Gaonkar (Sr. Developer Advocate at Amazon Web Services (AISPL))

Rohini frequently speaks at conferences, community meetups, and online events and for us she would be speaking about
"Getting Started with Serverless Architectures"

Serverless architectures let you build and deploy applications and services with infrastructure resources that require zero administration. In the past, you had to provision and scale servers to run your application code, install and operate distributed databases, and build and run custom software to handle API requests. Now, AWS provides a stack of scalable, fully-managed services that eliminates these operational complexities. In this session, you will learn about serverless architectures, their benefits, and the basics of the AWS’s serverless stack (e.g., AWS Lambda, Amazon API Gateway, and AWS Step Functions).
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Meet our second speaker
Shraddha Kudalkar (Software Engineer at Udgama)
She will tell us "What exactly OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect is?"
She will cover
- Introduction to OAuth and OpenID Connect
- What problem we were facing and what problem it solves
- OAuth and OpenID flow.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Meet our third speaker
Neha Kumari
Senior Software Engineer in the Search & Recommendation team at Flipkart

Topic -
Improving product discovery via Hierarchical Recommendations!
Abstract -

A recommendation engine’s primary goal is to surface personalised & relevant content to the user, content which satisfies explicit intent as well as serendipitous content that would otherwise be invisible. E-commerce categories such as Lifestyle, have a lot of flux, the trends last for a short time window and have their demand distributed across an extensive selection. In such cases, recommending product collections can be a better idea instead of individual products.

In this talk I will be talking about the recommendation system at Flipkart, our journey towards recommending collections and how it improved the product discovery and helped in solving cold start problems. I will also cover the relevance algorithm used which is a hybrid of collaborative and content-based recommendation, and how this is achieved at scale, where we have a catalogue consisting of ~400M products and this will be ever growing in this ecommerce world.

Bio -
I am a Senior Software Engineer in the Search & Recommendation team at Flipkart. I have worked on building scalable systems for product recommendations and personalisation. In the past I have also worked on Natural Language Processing. I have graduated from IIT BHU.

Linkedin : https://www.linkedin.com/in/nehaskumari/
Medium : https://medium.com/@_neha_kumari
Stackoverflow : https://stackoverflow.com/users/5488173/neha-kumari
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
We also have one flash talk 👇
Saloni Mhapsekar(https://twitter.com/salonimhapsekar) about her recent open source and social cause project Typedtales she is working on.

Talk Title: Including the Excluded

Abstract: While accessibility is the quality that makes an experience open to all, inclusive design is a design methodology that enables and draws on the full range of human diversity. TypedTales is an open source project which provides free social and emotional education to children worldwide. The platform is built by designing for one i.e Autism

Spectrum disorder and making it accessible for all. With TypedTales, we are trying to help people with learning differences and their families to
deal with the new lifestyles attributed to COVID-19 pandemic. The new normal has forced us to look for new ways to teach, learn and grow.

title: Mumbai Women Coder Meetup
description:
There is no better way to make this beautiful weather in Mumbai more exciting, than to come together and discuss amazing ideas, learn something new, meet amazing people and in turn grow our community.

With that said, Mumbai Women Coders is back again with an exciting meetup in the month of July. The meetup is never possible without someone igniting and sharing their idea, so here we announce the speakers and there topics

1. Sayalee Pote : CoreML in iOS

Talk will start with a very brief explanation of what machine learning actually is.This will be followed by an introduction to CoreML, which is Apple’s Machine Learning framework for iOS. This is will be a detailed explanation of functioning of CoreML, where does CoreML fit in the iOS App structure and various libraries in CoreML (Vision, Natural Language Processing, GameplayKit). The talk will proceed to show what it takes to integrate CoreML in your iOS app. This includes system setup and various ways in which you can feed a trained model to the iOS app to work with.

2. Arwa Lokhandwala : Lets make your javascript journey easy

This talk aims at giving you some very handy tips and few coding practices to make your javascript programming fun and interesting. At the same time it would help you to improve your code quality.

3. Avinash : Painless refactoring with selectors

Selectors are the way of computing derived data from redux stores.

4. Interactive session with Attendees : How did you get started with coding?

Its an interactive session, where we would like you to share what is the best coding practice that helped you, don't worry if you are beginners, share your coding ideas so that we can all get better!!

Requirement : As our meetup is open to all kinds of tech, please come with an open mind :)

title: International Women's Day Tech Meetup - Women Techmakers Mumbai
description:
NOTE: First preference will be given to ladies

Hello TechLadies,

International Women’s Day is a global reminder to all the ladies about their achievement, their strength and we wish to do the same in Mumbai.

Join us on 10th March to celebrate it with all the beautiful and talented techies from Mumbai. We’ve amazing tech enthusiastic women speakers who will be sharing their knowledge and tech journey with you all. As we've got the limited number of seats for the event ( Max 60 ) we request you to RSVP ASAP.

We’re grateful for Google’s Women Techmakers for sponsoring food and swag for the event and also Xoriant Solutions for providing the space for the event.

We strongly believe that there are many tech enthusiastic women in Mumbai who are learning, growing and supporting each other every day. We’re hoping that you’ll come forward to this event and share your story along with the piece of Pizza! :)

We are excited to announce the speakers for the event,

1) Deepa Naik is a co-founder at Cogitari, a HR and Technology Consulting firm and a founding member of Humans For AI, a global non-profit organization focused on building a more diverse workforce for the future leveraging AI technologies.

She is going to talk about Imbalanced data sets in AI and how its impacting bias in AI products. She will emphasize on gender bias and diversity and its significance in building better teams and AI products.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/deepa-naik

2)Tejshree Yeole is working with Network18 as an Android Developer. She has 5+ years of experience. Exploring latest things, guiding & inspiring people who love development is part and parcel of her life.
She is going to talk about building better apps using Android’s Performance Tooling. What is App’s Quality?How to measure it? Is it the UI rendering time, or the API call time?How to find out what is the exact issue & how to fix it.

https://twitter.com/tejshree_yeole
https://www.linkedin.com/in/tejshree-yeole-86a7763b/

3 ) Deesha Vora Co-founder and CPO @SuperWiseSite is going talk about "Persistence always Persists"
When we are in deadlock situation while coding. Where we go? StackOverflow, Github solutions, Techie friends and the list goes on. But behind the scene what we really do? We Persist.
https://twitter.com/deesharv

4 )Akanksha Sharma working with Network18 as iOS Developer. With almost 5 years of experience in Mobile and Web Development, she is going to talk about "Declarative Programming, the Concept and its use in Swift"
https://twitter.com/akanksharmaa

5 ) Arwa Lokhandwala is developer @BookMyShow is going to talk about “Why the hell React?🤔”
https://twitter.com/arwa18793

Flash Talks:
1) Anuja Kothekar is an Android application developer having 2+ year experience in IT industry. She loves to learn different technologies and work on them. When on break from coding, she can be found enjoying with Inkscape
She is going talk about "Don't let your App Dozzzzzzzzzze"
https://twitter.com/anujakothekar

2) Disha Mourya is Google certified Associate Android Developer having 2+ years of hands-on experience in development. She attends every possible tech meetups be it for Android or other technologies. Her Topic is "Explore, Learn, Share, Repeat!"
Networking is incredibly important to moving forward in one’s career, especially in tech circles, where everything moves so quickly and one has to be updated with new concepts of their respective field or programming language. So, In this talk, she wants to encourage women developers to attend tech meetups and share her personal experience with various tech meetups along with the one organized by Google i.e. GDD India 2017
https://twitter.com/disha4mourya

3) Ranjana Singh 15+ years of experience in HR domain with specific expertise in strategy development for people skills would to talk about "Power of Subconscious Mind"

4) Rakhi Gaikwad has 15+ years of experience in QA with Manual and Automation expertise and would like to share latest QA trend & Challenges in IT Industry.

5) Pankaj Yadav is Devops with 15+ years of experience is going share her professional journey with DevOps and why DevOps called new era of IT industry.

6)Ritu Rungta is Marketing Head with 13+ years of experience going talk about Understanding how marketing extends beyond the conventional team of marketing and how everyone in a professional field is a marketer for oneself and for the organization. Few trends that is picking for young professionals and relevant today.

See you all on 10th
Cheers!

title: IoT Azure workshop
description:
Join us for a fun workshop on Azure IoT. You get to play around with Raspberry Pi (both 2 & 3) boards along with sensors.

Speaker: Gandhali Samant

Bio: Gandhali is a Sr. Technology evangelist from Microsoft India. Spending over 15 years in IT, Gandhali has worked with several MNCs in India and abroad, primarily in the insurance and retail domain. In her current role as a certified cloud architect, she helps partners, customers and developers in building world-class solutions on Microsoft’s Cloud platform. Her primary focus is on working with startups and helping them to move to cloud. A graduate in Computer Engineering from the University Of Mumbai, Gandhali holds a Master’s degree in Computer Science from the Florida Institute Of Technology.

You can connect with her on twitter @s_gandhali and her blog link is http://blogs.msdn.com/b/gsamant/

Target Audience: IoT enthusiasts

Prerequisites :

A Microsoft Account (https://signup.live.com/signup?wa=wsignin1.0&wtrealm=urn%3afederation%3aMicrosoftOnline&wctx=estsredirect%3d2%26estsrequest%3drQIIAbPSyigpKSi20tdPTE7OL80r0SvPzEvJLy9OrCotStVLzs_Vyy9Kz0wBsaLYgYJJmTk5SYyGRUJcAmLrxFztxbQ8lgpv33hueUDTLEaOnMwysKZVjOqEzNUvzkzPKy24wMj4gpGxi4nF0MDYeBMTS7BjgOcJpuaTcreYBP2L0j1TwovdUlNSixJLMvPzHjHxhhanFvnn5VSG5Gen5k1i5svJT8_Miy8uSotPy8kvBwoAjS5ITC6JL8lMzk4t2cWskpJsnGxgbpika5yUaKhrYphipJuYlmiga5poAQSWZmZpKWkHWDZwXmARuMXCmppYnGn0g4VxESvQhxqLpr74edrWuV_Ibnf9pkUJuzjLifSWfX5aWmqRbW6xbmJVka6BgYlJgVpiQYFniq2hgZFacn5RUWoO2EtAkRTLtESjlDQT3VSLZKADE82NdZMSjUx0zVNSjYwTzQxTkk0sAQ2&id=&cbcxt=azubill&lw=1&fl=easi2&bk=1464750339&uaid=c265c2bf834e40358a580856b5f75fea&cru=https%3a%2f%2flogin.live.com%2flogin.srf%3fwa%3dwsignin1.0%26wtrealm%3durn%253afederation%253aMicrosoftOnline%26wctx%3destsredirect%253d2%2526estsrequest%253drQIIAbPSyigpKSi20tdPTE7OL80r0SvPzEvJLy9OrCotStVLzs_Vyy9Kz0wBsaLYgYJJmTk5SYyGRUJcAmLrxFztxbQ8lgpv33hueUDTLEaOnMwysKZVjOqEzNUvzkzPKy24wMj4gpGxi4nF0MDYeBMTS7BjgOcJpuaTcreYBP2L0j1TwovdUlNSixJLMvPzHjHxhhanFvnn5VSG5Gen5k1i5svJT8_Miy8uSotPy8kvBwoAjS5ITC6JL8lMzk4t2cWskpJsnGxgbpika5yUaKhrYphipJuYlmiga5poAQSWZmZpKWkHWDZwXmARuMXCmppYnGn0g4VxESvQhxqLpr74edrWuV_Ibnf9pkUJuzjLifSWfX5aWmqRbW6xbmJVka6BgYlJgVpiQYFniq2hgZFacn5RUWoO2EtAkRTLtESjlDQT3VSLZKADE82NdZMSjUx0zVNSjYwTzQxTkk0sAQ2%26id%3d%26cbcxt%3dazubill%26lw%3d1%26fl%3deasi2%26lc%3d1033&mkt=EN-US&lc=1033&sl=1&lic=1) (Hotmail, Live, Outlook.Com).

Visual Studio dev essentials free azure credits

https://www.visualstudio.com/dev-essentials/ )

A GitHub (https://github.com/) account.

Software required: (We will look at authoring Bots using c# and nodejs SDKs)

venue.name: Microsoft Corporation India Pvt. Limited
venue.address: Kalina, Off CST Road, 4th Floor, BKC, Windsor, Santa Cruz East, Mumbai – 400098

title: Full day Windows Azure Codelab Know Your Cloud
description:
https://secure.meetupstatic.com/photos/event/6/7/3/b/600_461966427.jpeg

Building applications with the ASP.NET stack should feel normal and fundamental to your life as a developer.

But in days before, developers had to focus on development along with hosting and maintenance such as scaling of web application when load is increased or implement load-balancing to improve performance of compute time or to manage multiple environment such as production , development and staging .

In 3 hours, Krunal Trivedi (Microsoft Certified Trainer) will get you started with full stack ASP.NET MVC or Web Form application development using Visual Studio and host it on Windows Azure .

You will create a database on Azure , connect it with our web application. We will then create multiple environments like development , testing and production. Also Krunal will show you how to manual scale and auto-scale your web application.

In the post lunch , 2 hours session He will guide you how to implement security and identity providers using Azure Active Directory.

Should I attend?

You should come if you are a...

Beginner Dot net developers with MVC or Web Forms stack who wants to get familiar with Windows Azure and Platform-as-a-Service offering of Azure.

Intermediate developer who want to understand most crucial and important concept of Azure Active Directory and hot wo connect your on-prem Active Directory to Azure Active Directory.

Assets provided at the event

Wifi

Source Code for all apps

Coffee (slurp)

What you need to bring

Working laptop with Visual Studio 2013 installed with SQL Server Express edition. (not compulsory. You can learn just by watching me code). If you don’t have Visual Studio 2013 just bring your laptop , we will create Visual Studio in cloud under Virtual Image (either way , you get a lot of learning folks :)

Basic working knowledge of ASP.NET Web Forms / MVC Application (I will cover some basics of Entity Framework )

If you have worked with Windows Active Directory, will help you to understand Azure Active Directory .(If you don’t have working knowledge of it then no worries.. “Main Hoon Na….”) said by Kunal :)

What we will cover (Agenda)

A quick look at the Windows Azure covering PaaS, IaaS and SaaS (30 mins)

• What is Azure?

• Why we need Azure?

• What are different offerings of Azure?

• Where your data is actually hosted?

• Services provided by Azure.

• Getting your Development Environment ready in Azure Virtual Machine(20 mins)

• Creating ASP.NET Webform / MVC Application (10 Mins)

• Create SQL Server Database to work with (10 mins)

• Creating Azure Web Apps template in the Cloud and understanding different configuration options. (20 mins)

• Hosting your web applications on cloud along with CRUD operations using Entity Framework (20 mins)

• Implementing manual and auto-scale with matrix and schedule

• Generating Production , Development and Staging slot and switching between slots.

• How to implement security using Azure Active Directory and how to connect your on-premises Active Directory to the Cloud.

What you will learn (Key Takeaways)

You will learn how to:

• Develop and host web applications and database to the cloud.

• How to implement saling and create multiple instances of your web app.

• How to create virtual machine in the cloud and connect to it.

• How to implement security and identity provider using Azure Active Directory.

• How to connect on-premises Active Directory to the Cloud.

Meet the Speaker

We have Krunal Trivedi who delivers training on various Microsoft technologies like .NET Framework , ASP.NET,MVC Framework , SharePoint Server , Windows Azure – PaaS and IaaS,Office 365.

He works as an IT Corporate Trainer since 8 years and has trained thousands of participants in various software MNCs such as Accenture-Avanade , JPMC , DataMatics , SONATA Software , Fidelity Information Services etc,HCL , ISM , Excellence Infocom,Prime Focus Technology etc..

He writes articles on his personal website www.techtrainingpoint.com (http://www.techtrainingpoint.com/) on various Microsoft as well as JavaScript technologies including Angularjs, Angular 2, React JS and Knockout JS.

Hey TechGeeks,

It's gonna be a fun session power-packed with immense knowledge. Watch us explore some of the cool technologies of today. We've awesome speakers, who'll make tech jargons easy for you to understand.

Speaker: Amit Erandole (Senior Engineer & Public Speaker)

Write to me at dube.manjula668[at]gmail[dot]com for any queries
